process_name: Flavor-changing neutral-current top decay t -> c g
implementation_difficulty_reason: Limit cataloging is MEDIUM, but production-grade integration is HIGH because the repo lacks a top chromomagnetic FCNC operator convention, RS-to-SMEFT matching, t -> c g width and c g -> t production normalization, collider/PDF reinterpretation machinery, and QCD-running choices for this operator.

evidence: Required plan greps rerun on 2026-05-16; no source-code implementation of t -> c g, tcg, tqg, C_uG, or c g -> t found.
Targeted implementation grep found only generic FCNC prose/comment hits at quarkConstraints/PAPER_0710_1869.md:35 and tests/test_paper_couplings.py:258, not an observable implementation.
quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23 enumerates only epsilon_K, K, B_d, B_s, and D0 in the modern quark policy surface.
quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:165 says the modern surface is policy-only and does not define a numeric EFT backend.
grep_commands: rg -n "epsilon_K|epsilon_k|Delta m_K|DELTA_M_K|evaluate_delta_mk|B_d|b_d|B_s|b_s|D0|d_mix|mu.?e.?gamma|muToEGamma|BR_LIMIT|MEG" quarkConstraints flavorConstraints scanParams neutrinos yukawa qcd scripts tests docs -g "!*.ipynb"

rg -n "b.?->.?s|X_s|K\\*|R_K|R_D|B_s.*mu|pi.*nu|nu.*nubar|epsilon.?prime|eps.?prime|t.?->|Z.?->|h.?->|EDM|dipole|conversion|tau.?->|mu.?->.?3e" quarkConstraints flavorConstraints scanParams neutrinos yukawa qcd scripts tests docs -g "!*.ipynb"

rg -n "MODERN_PHENOMENOLOGY_SYSTEM_IDS|DEFAULT_DELTA_F2_INPUTS|evaluate_bd_mixing|evaluate_bs_mixing|evaluate_d0_mixing|check_mu_to_e_gamma" quarkConstraints flavorConstraints scanParams tests docs -g "!*.ipynb"

rg -n "t.?->.?c.?g|t.?->.?g.?c|tcg|tgc|c.?g.?->.?t|tqg|uG|ctg|FCNC|flavou?r-changing|flavor changing" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g "!*.ipynb"

status_history.reason: Initial PKA draft and source snapshot deposit.
Writer polish for wa_w5a_top_higgs_ew; numerical claims checked against sidecar/source snapshots.

issues: CHK-1: TeX numerical claims for the SM B(t -> c g)=4.6e-12 benchmark and CFW 21/33 TeV RS context are outside pdg_or_equivalent.values; the CA checklist requires every TeX numerical physics claim to appear in pdg_or_equivalent with complete metadata.

status_history.reason: Cycle-2 writer rework: mirrored CA-flagged SM and CFW numerical context claims into pdg_or_equivalent.values with complete metadata.
Cycle-2 checker re-check passed CHK-1 through CHK-8 after WA-v2 metadata updates.
Opus round 2 per-process sign-off; CA-verified PASS retained; see flavor_catalog/signoff/round_002_index.md.

status_history.notes: Fetched cited PDG/arXiv/collaboration sources; measured value claims match.

status_history.note: B: down-sector alignment promotes up-sector observables

sections.process:
The observable is the flavor-changing neutral-current top decay
\(\mathcal{B}(t\to c g)\), catalog id T005 in the top/Higgs/electroweak
family.  Experimentally it is constrained mainly through anomalous single-top
production, \(c g\to t\), then translated into an equivalent top-decay
branching fraction under a specified chromomagnetic-operator convention.
sections.pdg_prose:
The source-of-truth values are in the \texttt{pdg\_or\_equivalent} block of
\texttt{T005.yaml}.  The canonical experimental source is the PDG/pdgLive
Q007TUG top-FCNC gluon datablock, accessed on 2026-05-16
(\texttt{PDG2025TopFCNCTUG};
\texttt{references/T005/pdg\_2025\_top\_tug\_tcg.txt}).  Its ATLAS Run-2
entry, AAD 2022T, uses \(139~\mathrm{fb}^{-1}\) of \(13~\mathrm{TeV}\)
\(pp\) data (\texttt{ATLAS2022TopFCNCTUG}).  For the charm-gluon production
mode the PDG block gives
\[
  \sigma(cg\to t)\,{\cal B}(t\to bW)\,{\cal B}(W\to \ell\nu)<4.7~\mathrm{pb}
\]
at 95\% CL, interpreted in the EFT basis as
\[
  |C^{ct}_{uG}|/\Lambda^2 < 0.14~\mathrm{TeV}^{-2},
  \qquad
  {\cal B}(t\to c g)<3.7\times10^{-4}.
\]
(\texttt{PDG2025TopFCNCTUG}; \texttt{ATLAS2022TopFCNCTUG}).
The same PDG block records the CMS 7 and 8 TeV comparison result based on
\(5.0~\mathrm{fb}^{-1}\) and \(19.7~\mathrm{fb}^{-1}\):
\(|\kappa_{tcg}|/\Lambda<1.8\times10^{-2}~\mathrm{TeV}^{-1}\), corresponding to
\({\cal B}(t\to c g)<4.1\times10^{-4}\)
(\texttt{CMS2017TopFCNCTUG}).  For SM context, not as a measured PDG value,
Aguilar-Saavedra gives
\({\cal B}(t\to c g)=4.6\times10^{-12}\) as the central SM benchmark
(\texttt{AguilarSaavedra2004TopFCNC}).
sections.relevance:
This channel probes color-carrying top-charm flavor violation rather than the
neutral-meson \(\Delta F=2\) four-quark operators already audited in the quark
scan.  In anarchic warped flavor, the heavy color sector and the IR-localized
top can generate non-universal mass-basis couplings and loop-level
chromomagnetic dipoles.  A \(t\to c g\) limit is therefore a direct up-sector
top-flavor diagnostic, complementary to \(D^0\) mixing and to electroweak
channels such as \(t\to cZ\) or \(t\to cH\).
sections.post_2008:
The 2008 CFW reference point emphasized KK-gluon exchange and generic
anarchic-flavor bounds near \(21~\mathrm{TeV}\) for ordinary RS and
\(33~\mathrm{TeV}\) in the composite pseudo-Goldstone Higgs setup, but it did
not provide a direct \(t\to c g\) catalog limit
(\texttt{CsakiFalkowskiWeiler2008WarpedFlavor}).  Since then the important
change is experimental: LHC searches now constrain the top-gluon FCNC operator
directly.  CMS used 7 and 8 TeV t-channel single-top data
(\texttt{CMS2017TopFCNCTUG}), while ATLAS later used the full Run-2
\(139~\mathrm{fb}^{-1}\) dataset at 13 TeV with a neural-network separation of
\(ug\to t\), \(cg\to t\), and backgrounds
(\texttt{ATLAS2022TopFCNCTUG}).  The resulting limits remain many orders of
magnitude above the SM prediction, so they are new-physics limits rather than
SM precision tests.
sections.validity:
The experimental upper bound is clean in the sense that the SM rate is
negligible.  Its use as an RS scan cut is model dependent: ATLAS quotes a
left-handed SMEFT \(C_{uG}^{ct}\) interpretation, while CMS uses an older
\(\kappa_{tcg}\) convention.  The conversion from single-top production to
\({\cal B}(t\to c g)\) assumes one FCNC operator at a time, standard top
decays, and a specific normalization of the chromomagnetic interaction.  A
model producing both chiralities, contact terms, or correlated heavy-gluon
resonance effects needs a dedicated reinterpretation.

In down-aligned or kaon-protected RS variants, top FCNCs become leading rather
than secondary diagnostics.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
\textbf{NO.}  The required plan greps were rerun on 2026-05-16, followed by a
targeted search for \(t\to c g\), \(tcg\), \(tqg\), \(C_{uG}\), and
\(cg\to t\) across \texttt{quarkConstraints/}, \texttt{qcd/},
\texttt{flavorConstraints/}, \texttt{neutrinos/}, \texttt{yukawa/},
\texttt{warpConfig/}, \texttt{solvers/}, \texttt{scanParams/}, and
\texttt{tests/}.  The only implementation-directory hits were generic FCNC
prose/comments at \texttt{quarkConstraints/PAPER\_0710\_1869.md:35} and
\texttt{tests/test\_paper\_couplings.py:258}.  The modern phenomenology surface
enumerates only \(\epsilon_K\), \(K\), \(B_d\), \(B_s\), and \(D^0\) at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23}, and states that it is
policy-only at \texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:165}.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{HIGH.}  Cataloging the external limit is straightforward, but live
integration would need a top chromomagnetic FCNC operator convention,
RS-to-SMEFT matching, decay-width and \(cg\to t\) production normalization,
PDF/collider acceptance assumptions or a validated recast, and QCD-running
choices not present in the existing \(\Delta F=2\) code.
